title: Fix every seed before trusting a result
description: Seed Python, NumPy, and the framework RNGs and set deterministic flags so a reported improvement is real, not run-to-run noise.
body: Use when comparing two configurations or reporting an improvement. Do not conclude that a change helped from a single unseeded run — initialization and shuffling variance can exceed the effect. Actions: set seeds for the language RNG, NumPy, and the framework (and enable deterministic kernels where available), then run each config across several seeds and compare distributions. Validation signals include a reproduced metric under the same seed and a mean improvement larger than the seed-to-seed standard deviation. Risk boundary: shipping a change justified by noise wastes compute and misleads; require multi-seed evidence before claiming a win.
